title: 9分达人听力7 Test 5 Listening
source: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析7》

groups.title: Employment Agency: Possible Jobs
groups.desc:
Questions 1-6
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: First Job • Job title: $1$ • Type of magazine: weekly $2$ magazine • Hours per week: $3$ hours • Starting salary: £$4$ a month Second Job • Job title: $5$ • Hours per week: 45 hours • Starting salary: £7200 a month • Source of information: $6$

questions.title: Job title: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 first job，提示词为 job title，此处应填职业类名词。
题目解析：本题作为开篇第一题，定位难度不大。在女士和男士两人相互介绍后，听到“The first one is ...”，提示答案即将出现，准备抓取。接着听到职业类词汇 reporter，记录下来即可。本题无干扰信息，难度不大。

questions.title: Type of magazine: weekly 【   】 magazine
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 magazine，提示词为 type、weekly、magazine，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过男士提问“What sort of magazine is it?”定位到该题。女士回答“They're called Home Solutions, which publishes articles on housing every week ...”，其中 every week 对应题干的 weekly，housing 说明该杂志类型为 housing。注意，原文中的 Home Solutions 是杂志的名称而非类型，并且其也不符合题目的字数要求，因此不能填写。

questions.title: Hours per week: 【   】 hours
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 per week、hours，此处应填数字。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过男士提问“What are the hours of work?”定位到该题。女士回答“... you will be expected to work forty hours a week ...”，forty 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。注意，女士随后提到“... there may be opportunities to do overtime ... some may work more than fifty hours a week ... But you're not required to do this.”，可以判断 50 多个小时是有些加班的人的工作时间，不是必须的，属于干扰信息。

questions.title: Starting salary: £【   】 a month
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 salary，提示词为 starting、£、a month，此处应填数字。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过男士提问“Do you know what the rates of pay are?”定位到该题。女士回答“... you'll get a monthly pay of 7000 pounds for the first six months.”，其中 monthly 对应题干的 a month，for the first six months 对应 starting，可知 7000 为正确答案。注意，女士随后提到的 7500 pounds 是试用期后可能拿到的工资，属于干扰信息。

questions.title: Job title: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 second job，提示词为 job title，此处应填职业类名词。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文提到 second position，提示答案即将出现，准备抓取。接着听到职业类词汇 researcher，记录下来即可。本题无干扰信息，难度不大。

questions.title: Source of information: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 source of information，此处应填名词，表示信息来源。
题目解析：题目已给信息 45 hours、7200 pounds 出现后，通过女士提问“... may I ask where you heard about us?”定位到该题。女士提供的两个选择“Was it in an advert or did your friend tell you?”都被男士否定了“Neither”。男士回答“It was a website.”，可知 website 为正确答案。注意，随后出现的 phone book 是男士找电话号码的来源，不符合题干要求。

groups.question_title: What is the man's attitude towards the position of writer in the following different fields?
groups.desc:
Questions 7-10
What is the man's attitude towards the position of writer in the following different fields?
Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to Questions 7-10.

questions.title: Sports
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 sports。
题目解析：这组配对题定位难度不大，题目信息在原文皆原词重现。考生在审题时需注意三个选项的区别（very、slightly、not），在听的过程中重点抓取相关信息并对应各个选项即可。本题定位词 sports 由女士提出，定位之后，男士提到“I love sports. I'm a football fan.”。注意，句中男士表示感兴趣的是运动本身，而非写作，因此还不能确认答案。男士接着提到“But as far as writing is concerned, I'm not entirely keen on that. I think I'll rule it out.”，其中 not keen on、rule it out 可以判断男士对运动领域的写作并不感兴趣，因此 C 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: Entertainment
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 entertainment。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过女士提问 entertainment 定位到该题。男士回答“Entertainment is an exciting topic ... but I'm not sure if I should write something about it... I'll think about that.”，其中 not sure 和“I'll think about that.”表明他还不确定，要考虑一下，可以判断他是有点儿感兴趣的，因此 B 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: Soap drama
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 soap drama。
题目解析：通过女士提到的“... the third one is writing reviews for soap dramas.”定位到该题。男士回答“... soap drama is my favourite ... I think it's pretty worthwhile to give my own opinions and recommendations to the audience.”，其中 favourite 和 worthwhile 可以判断他对此非常感兴趣，因此 A 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: Business Economy magazine
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 business economy magazine。
题目解析：定位词 business economy magazine 原词重现后，男士回答“I'm unfamiliar with this field ... it might not be easy”，可知他的态度是偏否定的。接着女士进行了劝说“Don't worry too much ...”，男士随后回应“That sounds not too bad.”，说明他觉得还不错。之后女士说给他发宣传册了解更多细节，他也表示同意，可以判断他也有点儿感兴趣，因此 B 选项为正确答案。

transcript.en: Woman: Good morning.
transcript.cn: 早上好。

transcript.en: Woman: How may I help you?
transcript.cn: 有什么可以帮您的吗？

transcript.en: Man: Good morning.
transcript.cn: 早上好。

transcript.en: Man: We spoke on the phone last week, and I'm ringing to get more details about the job.
transcript.cn: 我们上周通过电话，我打来是想进一步了解这份工作的详情。

transcript.en: Woman: That's right.
transcript.cn: 没错。

transcript.en: Woman: Thank you for sending in your CV.
transcript.cn: 感谢您寄来简历。

transcript.en: Woman: We've carefully looked at it, and I think we have several jobs that might suit you.
transcript.cn: 我们已经仔细看过了，我觉得有几份工作可能适合您。

transcript.en: Man: That's great!
transcript.cn: 太好了！

transcript.en: Woman: The first one is in a magazine based in North London.
transcript.cn: 第一份工作是在一家总部位于伦敦北部的杂志社。

transcript.en: Woman: They're looking for a reporter.
transcript.cn: 他们正在招聘一名记者。

transcript.en: Man: What sort of magazine is it?
transcript.cn: 是什么类型的杂志？

transcript.en: Woman: They're called Home Solutions, which publishes articles on housing every week, and they want to find someone responsible for collecting the latest news and writing relevant articles.
transcript.cn: 杂志名叫《家居解决方案》，每周刊登住房方面的文章。他们想找一个负责收集最新消息并撰写相关文章的人。

transcript.en: Man: What are the hours of work?
transcript.cn: 工作时间是怎样的？

transcript.en: Woman: Well, you will be expected to work forty hours a week, but there may be opportunities to do overtime during busy periods.
transcript.cn: 嗯，您每周需要工作40小时，不过在繁忙时期可能有加班的机会。

transcript.en: Woman: For example, some may work more than fifty hours a week and get extra pay.
transcript.cn: 例如，有些人每周可能工作50多个小时，并获得额外报酬。

transcript.en: Woman: But you're not required to do this.
transcript.cn: 但这不是强制要求。

transcript.en: Man: That's reasonable.
transcript.cn: 这很合理。

transcript.en: Man: Do you know what the rates of pay are?
transcript.cn: 您知道薪资标准是多少吗？

transcript.en: Woman: Yes.
transcript.cn: 知道。

transcript.en: Woman: It says you'll get a monthly pay of 7000 pounds for the first six months.
transcript.cn: 上面说，前六个月您的月薪是7000英镑。

transcript.en: Woman: And after the probationary period, you'll be eligible for a salary of more than that, maybe 7500 pounds or more.
transcript.cn: 试用期结束后，您的薪水会高于这个数，可能是7500英镑或更多。

transcript.en: Man: That's good.
transcript.cn: 那不错。

transcript.en: Woman: So, is that something that might interest you?
transcript.cn: 那么，您对这份工作感兴趣吗？

transcript.en: Man: It is, yes.
transcript.cn: 是的，感兴趣。

transcript.en: Man: The only thing is, you say they are in North London, so it would be a pretty long commute for me.
transcript.cn: 唯一的问题是，您说他们在伦敦北部，所以对我来说通勤路程会很长。

transcript.en: Woman: In that case, the second position might suit you better regarding location.
transcript.cn: 既然这样，从地点来看，第二个职位可能更适合您。

transcript.en: Woman: That's a researcher for an electronic engineering company in South London.
transcript.cn: 那是伦敦南部一家电子工程公司的研究员职位。

transcript.en: Man: Yes, that would be a lot closer.
transcript.cn: 是的，那就近多了。

transcript.en: Woman: Your CV shows you have relevant working experience in this field.
transcript.cn: 您的简历显示，您在这个领域有相关工作经验。

transcript.en: Man: Yes, I worked in the office of a computer engineering company for two years.
transcript.cn: 是的，我曾在一家计算机工程公司的办公室工作过两年。

transcript.en: Man: What about the working hours?
transcript.cn: 工作时间呢？

transcript.en: Woman: For this job, you'll have to work 45 hours a week and the starting salary is 7200 pounds for the six-month probationary period.
transcript.cn: 这份工作每周要工作45小时，六个月试用期内的起薪是7200英镑。

transcript.en: Man: That should be nice.
transcript.cn: 听起来不错。

transcript.en: Woman: I can arrange for you an interview if you're interested.
transcript.cn: 如果您感兴趣，我可以为您安排面试。

transcript.en: Woman: But before that, may I ask where you heard about us?
transcript.cn: 不过在此之前，我能问一下您是从哪里知道我们的吗？

transcript.en: Woman: Was it in an advert or did your friend tell you?
transcript.cn: 是看到广告，还是朋友告诉您的？

transcript.en: Man: Neither.
transcript.cn: 都不是。

transcript.en: Man: It was a website.
transcript.cn: 我是在一个网站上看到的。

transcript.en: Man: Then I just got your number from the phone book.
transcript.cn: 然后我从电话簿里查到了你们的号码。

transcript.en: Woman: Oh, right.
transcript.cn: 哦，明白了。

transcript.en: Woman: Apart from full-time jobs, we have some part-time work that might suit you.
transcript.cn: 除了全职工作，我们还有一些可能适合您的兼职工作。

transcript.en: Woman: Here several magazines are recruiting part-time writers.
transcript.cn: 这里有几家杂志正在招聘兼职撰稿人。

transcript.en: Woman: Are you interested?
transcript.cn: 您感兴趣吗？

transcript.en: Man: What kind of writer exactly?
transcript.cn: 具体是哪类撰稿人？

transcript.en: Man: I mean, what types of articles should I write about?
transcript.cn: 我是说，我应该写哪类文章？

transcript.en: Woman: Well, the first one is to do with sports reports.
transcript.cn: 嗯，第一份是撰写体育报道。

transcript.en: Woman: For this, you're expected to write sports articles twice weekly.
transcript.cn: 这份工作要求您每周写两篇体育文章。

transcript.en: Man: Well, I love sports.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我喜欢体育。

transcript.en: Man: I'm a football fan.
transcript.cn: 我是个足球迷。

transcript.en: Man: But as far as writing is concerned, I'm not entirely keen on that.
transcript.cn: 但说到写体育文章，我并不是特别感兴趣。

transcript.en: Man: I think I'll rule it out.
transcript.cn: 我想我会把它排除掉。

transcript.en: Woman: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Woman: Then how about writing something for entertainment?
transcript.cn: 那么，写娱乐方面的内容怎么样？

transcript.en: Woman: Does it appeal to you?
transcript.cn: 这对您有吸引力吗？

transcript.en: Man: Entertainment is an exciting topic.
transcript.cn: 娱乐是个很有意思的话题。

transcript.en: Man: I like reading news or articles in this category, but I'm not sure if I should write something about it.
transcript.cn: 我喜欢阅读这一类的新闻或文章，但不确定自己是否应该写这方面的内容。

transcript.en: Man: Anyway, I'll think about that.
transcript.cn: 不管怎样，我会考虑一下。

transcript.en: Man: Could you give me more details?
transcript.cn: 您能给我更多详细信息吗？

transcript.en: Woman: OK, I've got your email, and I will send the details to you later.
transcript.cn: 好的，我有您的电子邮箱，稍后会把详细信息发给您。

transcript.en: Man: Thanks.
transcript.cn: 谢谢。

transcript.en: Woman: And the third one is writing reviews for soap dramas.
transcript.cn: 第三份是为肥皂剧撰写评论。

transcript.en: Man: Oh, soap drama is my favourite.
transcript.cn: 哦，肥皂剧是我的最爱。

transcript.en: Man: I worked as a freelance writer in this field three years ago.
transcript.cn: 三年前，我曾在这个领域做过自由撰稿人。

transcript.en: Man: I think it's pretty worthwhile to give my own opinions and recommendations to the audience.
transcript.cn: 我觉得向观众提出自己的看法和建议非常有意义。

transcript.en: Woman: That's good.
transcript.cn: 那很好。

transcript.en: Woman: And the last one is writing articles for a business economy magazine.
transcript.cn: 最后一份是为一本商业经济杂志撰写文章。

transcript.en: Man: I'm unfamiliar with this field, so I think it might not be easy.
transcript.cn: 我不熟悉这个领域，所以觉得可能不太容易。

transcript.en: Woman: Don't worry too much.
transcript.cn: 不用太担心。

transcript.en: Woman: Experience is not essential for this job.
transcript.cn: 这份工作并不要求必须有经验。

transcript.en: Woman: They've got a training session for every recruit.
transcript.cn: 他们会为每位新员工提供培训。

transcript.en: Man: That sounds not too bad.
transcript.cn: 听起来还不错。

transcript.en: Woman: OK, would you like me to send you the pamphlet so that you can learn more about it?
transcript.cn: 好的，需要我把宣传册发给您，让您进一步了解吗？

transcript.en: Man: That will be nice.
transcript.cn: 那太好了。

transcript.en: Woman: OK, that's it.
transcript.cn: 好的，就这些。

transcript.en: Woman: I'll send you all the information later and ...
transcript.cn: 稍后我会把所有资料发给您，然后……

groups.title: Muna Island
groups.desc:
Questions 11-20
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: How much should visitors tip taxi drivers on the island?

options.title: A
options.content: as much as they feel right

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 taxi drivers，提示词为 how much、taxi drivers、on the island。
题目解析：本题作为开篇第一题，定位难度不大。原文开头为一小段背景介绍，接着通过“The first suggestion is about tips.”引出该题。原文提到“If you take a taxi, there is no set rate on how much you should leave to the driver ... It's up to you entirely”，其中 taxi、driver 分别出现，no set rate 和“It's up to you entirely”表明小费的金额不固定，乘客自己定就行，与 A 选项内容相符，因此 A 选项为正确答案。原文后面提到去理发店和餐厅吃饭需要付 10% 的小费，可判断 C 选项不符合题干要求，属于张冠李戴。

questions.title: What is mentioned about the medical service on the island?

options.title: B
options.content: Visitors should contact their insurance company.

options.title: C
options.content: Visitors have to find the hotel doctor.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 medical service，提示词为 on the island。
题目解析：定位词原词重现。根据原文描述，游客生病时有两个选择：1. 请酒店安排会说英语的医生；2. 去当地诊所。这两种方式都需要收费（not a complimentary service / have to prepare for the charge），与 A 选项内容相符，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。原文说请酒店安排医生是最快的方法，但并非必须这样做，因此 C 选项可以排除。原文后面提到在岛上看病很贵，建议游客买好保险再来（buy your insurance before you leave），与 B 选项内容不符，因此 B 选项也可以排除。

questions.title: The tour guide says the tap water
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 tap water。
题目解析：定位词原词重现。原文先提到 tap water is safe to drink，因此 B 选项可以排除。接着提到“... its weird taste because it contains minerals.”，其中 weird taste 对应 C 选项的 unusual taste，因此 C 选项为正确答案。此外，通过 it contains minerals 可以排除 A 选项。

questions.title: What point is made about bottled water?

options.title: B
options.content: The bottle is not always sealed.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 bottled water。
题目解析：定位词原词重现。原文先提到 you can buy bottled water sold in every shop，与 C 选项内容相悖，因此 C 选项可以排除。接着提到“... but you'd better make sure the sealed lid is untouched, as the locals often put tap water into the bottle.”，表明当地人经常把瓶盖打开，将自来水装进瓶子里，与 B 选项内容相符，因此 B 选项为正确答案。原文并未提及与价格相关的信息，因此 A 选项可以排除。

questions.title: What feature is mentioned about the bus on the island?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 bus，提示词为 on the island。
题目解析：定位词原词重现。原文先提到 there aren't many bus lines，与 A 选项内容相悖，因此 A 选项可以排除。接着提到 they're always on time，说明公交车很准时，因此 B 选项可以排除。最后，答案由转折句引出“However, some people complained that the road was so bumpy that they even got nausea and dizziness.”，通过道路颠簸、恶心和头晕可以判断公交车坐起来让人不舒服，与 C 选项内容相符，因此 C 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: How can visitors know the destination of the bus on the island?

options.title: A
options.content: find it in front of the bus

options.title: C
options.content: find it at the bus stop

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 destination，提示词为 on the island。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过“And another thing is ...”可以定位到该题。原文提到“... before you jump on the bus, enquire of the driver about where the bus will go.”，与 B 选项内容相符，因此 B 选项为正确答案。接着提到“Unlike most cities, here in Muna Island, you cannot find the destination shown in front of the bus or at the bus stop.”，通过 unlike、cannot 等否定信息可以判断 A、C 选项均为干扰项。

questions.title: What does the tour guide advise visitors to do before renting a car?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 advise、before。
题目解析：题干信息 rent a car 先出现，接着原文提到 taxi companies，但其对应信息是“... many visitors sometimes find nobody answering the phone, so leave this way when you are in a hurry.”，说明说话人并不推荐这个方法，因此 A 选项可以排除。然后原文提到“It's wise to compare several car rental companies online beforehand, as the price may vary a lot”，其中 it's wise to do sth. 对应题干的 advise，说明说话人认为提前比较不同租车公司的价格是明智的做法，与 C 选项内容相符，因此 C 选项为正确答案。最后原文提到“Booking via hotel may be overcharged, as it contains a 10% commission, so it's also not advisable.”说明说话人不推荐通过酒店预订，因此 B 选项可以排除。

questions.title: When they rent a car, it's important for visitors to know

options.title: B
options.content: what is included in the price.

options.title: C
options.content: fuel is included in the price.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 important。
题目解析：上一题结束后，答案句随后跟上，连接较快，通过 more importantly 可以定位到该题。原文提到“... don't forget to check with the company what the price covers in detail, like daily rental expenses, car insurance, commission etc.”，与 B 选项内容相符，因此 B 选项为正确答案。接着提到“It usually includes unlimited mileage too, but fuel is excluded.”，其中 unlimited mileage 和 fuel is excluded 分别与 A、C 选项相反，因此这两个选项均可排除。

questions.title: How can visitors find a taxi easily?

options.title: B
options.content: look for a taxi in advance

options.title: C
options.content: stop a taxi on the street

questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 find a taxi、easily。
题目解析：上一题结束后，题干中的 find a taxi 原词重现。原文提到“If you want to find a taxi quickly, it's not a good idea to reserve through the taxi company ...”，通过句中的否定信息 not 可以排除 A 选项。接着提到“The easiest way is ...”这一信息，提示答案即将出现，之后的“... just waving your hands on the street to stop the taxi.”，与 C 选项内容相符，因此 C 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: What is suggested by the tour guide about the taxi fee?

options.title: A
options.content: make sure no extra money is asked

options.title: B
options.content: check what is included in the fee

options.title: C
options.content: agree the price with the driver in advance

questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 taxi fee、suggested。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过 another thing is 可以定位到该题。原文提到“... before you jump in the taxi, you'd better negotiate with the driver about how much you're going to pay.”，与 C 选项内容相符。此外，后面还提到 confirm the price before you get in，可进一步确认 C 选项为正确答案。原文虽提到晚上从机场打出租车会很贵，但并未提及 A、B 选项的相关内容，因此这两个选项可以排除。

transcript.en: Hello, everyone!
transcript.cn: 大家好！

transcript.en: I'm Hazel, the guide from Travelite Agency.
transcript.cn: 我是Travelite旅行社的导游Hazel。

transcript.en: Since we mentioned the trip to Muna Island in the last program, we've received several online messages from those who have been there.
transcript.cn: 自从我们在上一期节目中提到穆纳岛之旅后，就收到了几位去过那里的人发来的网络留言。

transcript.en: As a result, we've collected some helpful dos and don'ts for new visitors, which will hopefully help you to know the island better and enjoy your stay to the fullest!
transcript.cn: 因此，我们为初次到访的游客整理了一些实用的注意事项，希望能帮助大家更好地了解这座岛，并充分享受在岛上的时光！

transcript.en: The first suggestion is about tips.
transcript.cn: 第一条建议与小费有关。

transcript.en: Although tipping is required in most circumstances in Muna Island, the amount you should give differs significantly.
transcript.cn: 尽管在穆纳岛的大多数情况下都需要给小费，但具体金额差别很大。

transcript.en: If you take a taxi, there is no set rate on how much you should leave to the driver.
transcript.cn: 如果您乘坐出租车，给司机多少小费并没有固定标准。

transcript.en: Someone may give a generous tip if he thinks the service is fantastic.
transcript.cn: 如果有人认为服务非常好，可能会给一笔丰厚的小费。

transcript.en: It's up to you entirely.
transcript.cn: 这完全由您自己决定。

transcript.en: However, if you go to the hairdresser's or enjoy a meal in a restaurant and the bill comes with no service charge, a 10% tip is advisable.
transcript.cn: 不过，如果您去理发店，或在餐厅用餐而账单中没有服务费，建议给10%的小费。

transcript.en: Muna Island has got relatively good medical service.
transcript.cn: 穆纳岛的医疗服务相对不错。

transcript.en: If you get sick, the quickest way is to ask the hotel to arrange an English-speaking doctor for you, but remember it's not a complimentary service.
transcript.cn: 如果您生病了，最快的办法是请酒店为您安排一位会说英语的医生，但要记住，这项服务并不是免费的。

transcript.en: The other alternative is to visit the local clinic, and you have to prepare for the charge as well.
transcript.cn: 另一种选择是去当地诊所就诊，您同样要准备支付费用。

transcript.en: I should remind you that the medical service on the island can be pricey, so it's better to buy your insurance before you leave.
transcript.cn: 我得提醒大家，岛上的医疗服务可能很昂贵，所以最好在出发前购买保险。

transcript.en: When you visit Muna Island, you'll see the notice nearly everywhere: tap water is safe to drink.
transcript.cn: 游览穆纳岛时，您几乎到处都能看到这样的告示：自来水可以安全饮用。

transcript.en: But you might not get used to its weird taste because it contains minerals.
transcript.cn: 但由于水中含有矿物质，您可能不习惯它那种奇怪的味道。

transcript.en: In this case, you can buy bottled water sold in every shop, but you'd better make sure the sealed lid is untouched, as the locals often put tap water into the bottle.
transcript.cn: 在这种情况下，您可以买到每家商店都有售的瓶装水，但最好确认密封瓶盖没有被动过，因为当地人经常把自来水装进瓶子里。

transcript.en: Then I'll focus on transportation in Muna Island.
transcript.cn: 接下来我会重点介绍穆纳岛的交通。

transcript.en: The bus is the most commonly used means of transportation for local residents.
transcript.cn: 公交车是当地居民最常使用的交通工具。

transcript.en: Although there aren't many bus lines, they're always on time.
transcript.cn: 虽然公交线路不多，但公交车总是准时。

transcript.en: However, some people complained that the road was so bumpy that they even got nausea and dizziness.
transcript.cn: 不过，有些人抱怨道路太颠簸，甚至会感到恶心和头晕。

transcript.en: As a result, it's not a good choice if you want to sit peacefully and relax on the way.
transcript.cn: 因此，如果您想在途中安静地坐着休息，公交车并不是一个好选择。

transcript.en: And another thing is, before you jump on the bus, enquire of the driver about where the bus will go.
transcript.cn: 还有一点，上车前要向司机询问公交车开往哪里。

transcript.en: Unlike most cities, here in Muna Island, you cannot find the destination shown in front of the bus or at the bus stop.
transcript.cn: 与大多数城市不同，在穆纳岛，您无法在公交车前方或公交站看到目的地信息。

transcript.en: Maybe you've found the bus service is not so enjoyable, in that case, you can always rent a car.
transcript.cn: 或许您已经发现公交服务不太舒适，这种情况下，您随时可以租车。

transcript.en: If you choose this way, I advise you to prepare well beforehand.
transcript.cn: 如果选择这种方式，我建议您事先做好充分准备。

transcript.en: Although some taxi companies provide car rental services, many visitors sometimes find nobody answering the phone, so leave this way when you are in a hurry.
transcript.cn: 虽然有些出租车公司提供租车服务，但许多游客有时会发现电话无人接听，所以着急时不要采用这种方式。

transcript.en: It's wise to compare several car rental companies online beforehand, as the price may vary a lot.
transcript.cn: 最好事先在网上比较几家汽车租赁公司，因为价格可能相差很大。

transcript.en: Booking via hotel may be overcharged, as it contains a 10% commission, so it's also not advisable.
transcript.cn: 通过酒店预订可能会被多收费，因为其中包含10%的佣金，所以也不建议这样做。

transcript.en: More importantly, don't forget to check with the company what the price covers in detail, like daily rental expenses, car insurance, commission etc.
transcript.cn: 更重要的是，别忘了向公司详细确认价格包含哪些项目，例如每日租金、汽车保险、佣金等。

transcript.en: It usually includes unlimited mileage too, but fuel is excluded.
transcript.cn: 价格通常还包括不限里程，但不包括燃油费。

transcript.en: If you don't want to drive, a taxi is the quickest and most comfortable way, although few are available on Muna Island.
transcript.cn: 如果您不想开车，出租车是最快、最舒适的出行方式，尽管穆纳岛上的出租车很少。

transcript.en: If you want to find a taxi quickly, it's not a good idea to reserve through the taxi company, as sometimes you'll have quite a wait, especially in the rush hour.
transcript.cn: 如果想尽快找到出租车，通过出租车公司预约并不是好办法，因为有时要等很久，尤其是在高峰时段。

transcript.en: The easiest way is just waving your hands on the street to stop the taxi.
transcript.cn: 最简单的办法就是在街上挥手拦车。

transcript.en: Otherwise, the driver will drive away directly after he drops off the guests.
transcript.cn: 否则，司机送下乘客后会直接开走。

transcript.en: Oh, another thing is, I have to remind you that there is no meter for taxis in Muna Island, so before you jump in the taxi, you'd better negotiate with the driver about how much you're going to pay.
transcript.cn: 哦，还有一点我必须提醒大家，穆纳岛的出租车没有计价器，所以在上车前，最好先和司机谈妥车费。

transcript.en: For example, if you take a taxi from the airport at night, it may cost you.
transcript.cn: 例如，如果您夜间从机场乘坐出租车，可能会花费……

transcript.en: So, confirm the price before you get in.
transcript.cn: 所以，上车前要确认好价格。

transcript.en: OK, that's all about the tips for Muna Island that I want to share today.
transcript.cn: 好的，以上就是我今天想和大家分享的穆纳岛旅行注意事项。

transcript.en: And are there any questions?
transcript.cn: 大家有什么问题吗？

groups.title: Possible Course Change
groups.desc:
Questions 21-24
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect:
Name of student: Sally Layman
Current course: $21$
Time started: $22$
Courses interested in: 1st choice $23$
2nd choice $24$

questions.title: Current course: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Sally Layman，提示词为 current、course，此处应填课程类名词或名词短语。
题目解析：本题难度不大，无干扰信息。定位词 Sally Layman 出现后，男士提问“What course are you doing at present?”，其中题干信息 current 通过 at present 体现，替换难度不大。接着女生回答 Economic History，记录下来即可。注意，答案词首字母小写也可以，听力目前不再考查大小写问题。

questions.title: Time started: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 time、started，此处应填时间。
题目解析：本题无干扰信息。上一题结束后，即可听到提问“... when did you begin this course?”，据此可以定位到该题。接着女生回答 last September，记录下来即可，注意不要漏写 last。

questions.title: Courses interested in: 1st choice 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 courses、interested、1st choice，此处应填课程类名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，男士提问“... I want to know what courses you'd like to change to.”，据此可以定位到该题。接着女生回答“... my first choice would be Politics.”，Politics 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。注意，答案词首字母小写也可以，听力目前不再考查大小写问题。

questions.title: 2nd choice 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 interested、2nd choice，此处应填课程类名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，男士提到“But it's better to give another option.”，据此可以定位到该题。女生的回答中先出现课程类名词短语 Business Studies，但随即就进行了否定 but then I realize it is new to me，接着提到“... I think Philosophy might be a better choice.”，因此可以排除 Business Studies，确认 Philosophy 为正确答案。注意，答案词首字母小写也可以，听力目前不再考查大小写问题。

groups.desc:
Questions 25-28
Complete the table below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

questions.title: Find 【   】
questions.answer: lectures (are) difficult / lectures are difficult / lectures difficult
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 problem，本题无法准确预判答案词词性，需选取描述课程问题的信息填写。
题目解析：本题定位不难，通过男士提问引出题目“... let's talk about your problems. What do you think is your significant difficulty in the course?”。接着女生回答“... the biggest problem is that the lectures are difficult.”，lectures (are) difficult 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。

questions.title: Join other students in a 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 recording the lectures、other students，提示词为 join ... in、a，此处应填单数名词或名词短语。
题目解析：建议通过前文信息 recording the lectures 辅助定位。男士提到“... but I also advise you to take part in the discussion group with other fellow students ...”，其中 take part in 对应题干 join ... in，discussion group 符合要求，记录下来即可。

questions.title: Problem: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 problem，本题无法准确预判答案词词性，需选取描述课程问题的信息填写。
题目解析：上一题结束后，男士提问“And any other problems?”，据此可以定位到该题。接着女生回答“... the second problem is, I think there aren't enough tutorials ...”，可判断 aren't enough tutorials 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。

questions.title: Further strategies suggested: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 more background reading，提示词为 further strategies suggested，本题无法准确预判答案词词性，需选取描述策略建议的信息填写。
题目解析：虽然本题和上一题之间间隔时间较长，但是考生耐心听取，便可听到 talking to tutor、arrange meetings in advance、bad assignment 等信息的对应信息。当听到 more background reading 时，可知该题即将出现，准备抓取。男士提到“... I think you can try another strategy, which is to visit student services ...”，visit student services 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。注意，25-28 题的难点在于答案字数较多，单词拼写较长，考生需注意书写速度。

groups.desc:
Questions 29-30
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
Date for next meeting: $29$ at 3.30 p.m.
Meet with: $30$

questions.title: Date for next meeting: 【   】 at 3.30 p.m.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 date，此处应填日期。
题目解析：本题需通过女生提问定位，听到“... when shall I come back?”时，准备抓取时间类信息，接着听到男士回答 17th of February，记录下来。随后听到 half past three，符合题干要求，可确认答案。该题无干扰信息，难度不大，注意月份 February 的拼写。

questions.title: Meet with: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 meet with，此处应填跟人相关的名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，女生提问“And where shall I meet you?”，据此可以定位到该题。男士回答“But I'll leave this record to our senior adviser, and you will meet with him then in this office.”，其中 senior adviser 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。注意 adviser/advisor 都算正确。

transcript.en: Sally: Excuse me, Professor.
transcript.cn: 打扰一下，教授。

transcript.en: Sally: Can I talk to you for a minute?
transcript.cn: 我能和您谈一会儿吗？

transcript.en: Tutor: Yes, do come in.
transcript.cn: 可以，请进。

transcript.en: Tutor: So you're Sally Layman?
transcript.cn: 你是Sally Layman，对吗？

transcript.en: Sally: That's it.
transcript.cn: 是的。

transcript.en: Tutor: You want to discuss with me changing courses, right?
transcript.cn: 你想和我讨论转专业的事，对吗？

transcript.en: Sally: Yes.
transcript.cn: 是的。

transcript.en: Tutor: All right.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Tutor: What course are you doing at present, Sally?
transcript.cn: Sally，你目前读的是什么专业？

transcript.en: Sally: It's Economic History.
transcript.cn: 经济史。

transcript.en: Tutor: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Tutor: And when did you begin this course?
transcript.cn: 你是什么时候开始读这个专业的？

transcript.en: Sally: Let me think.
transcript.cn: 让我想想。

transcript.en: Sally: Erm ... it was last September.
transcript.cn: 嗯……是去年九月。

transcript.en: Tutor: Right.
transcript.cn: 好。

transcript.en: Tutor: Good.
transcript.cn: 很好。

transcript.en: Tutor: Before we talk through all your problems with the course, I want to know what courses you'd like to change to.
transcript.cn: 在详细讨论你在这门专业中遇到的所有问题之前，我想知道你希望转到什么专业。

transcript.en: Sally: Well, there're several courses that I've thought about, but my first choice would be Politics.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我考虑过几个专业，但我的第一选择是政治学。

transcript.en: Tutor: Oh, it's one of the most popular courses in our college.
transcript.cn: 哦，那是我们学院最热门的专业之一。

transcript.en: Sally: Yes, I know.
transcript.cn: 是的，我知道。

transcript.en: Sally: So it means there might not be places available?
transcript.cn: 所以这意味着可能没有名额了？

transcript.en: Tutor: That's right.
transcript.cn: 没错。

transcript.en: Tutor: I'll still make a note of that.
transcript.cn: 我还是会把它记下来。

transcript.en: Tutor: But it's better to give another option.
transcript.cn: 不过，最好再提供一个备选方案。

transcript.en: Sally: Well, I've thought of changing the course entirely to Business Studies, but then I realize it is new to me, so I think Philosophy might be a better choice.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我曾想彻底转去读商业研究，但后来意识到这个领域对我来说很陌生，所以我觉得哲学可能是更好的选择。

transcript.en: Tutor: OK, that's useful to know.
transcript.cn: 好的，知道这一点很有用。

transcript.en: Tutor: Then let's talk through any problems you've been having at the moment and see if there is any possibility for us to offer help.
transcript.cn: 那么，我们来详细谈谈你目前遇到的问题，看看我们是否有可能提供帮助。

transcript.en: Tutor: After all, with all the efforts that you've made already, it'll be a pity to give up the present course and completely start a new one.
transcript.cn: 毕竟，你已经付出了这么多努力，如果放弃目前的专业，完全从头开始读一个新专业，那就太可惜了。

transcript.en: Sally: Yes, I agree with that.
transcript.cn: 是的，我也这么认为。

transcript.en: Sally: I've learnt something valuable about Economic History.
transcript.cn: 我从经济史中学到了一些有价值的东西。

transcript.en: Tutor: Good.
transcript.cn: 很好。

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, let's talk about your problems.
transcript.cn: 好，我们来谈谈你的问题。

transcript.en: Tutor: What do you think is your significant difficulty in the course?
transcript.cn: 你认为自己在这门专业中面临的主要困难是什么？

transcript.en: Sally: Well, I have to say the biggest problem is that the lectures are difficult.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我得说，最大的问题是课程讲座很难听懂。

transcript.en: Tutor: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Tutor: And have you tried something to overcome this problem?
transcript.cn: 你尝试过什么方法来克服这个问题吗？

transcript.en: Sally: Yes, I have.
transcript.cn: 是的，尝试过。

transcript.en: Sally: I tried recording the lectures because I couldn't follow what the lecturer said.
transcript.cn: 因为我跟不上讲师讲的内容，所以我试着把讲座录下来。

transcript.en: Tutor: Yes, that's a good idea.
transcript.cn: 是的，这是个好主意。

transcript.en: Tutor: And did you find it helped?
transcript.cn: 你觉得有帮助吗？

transcript.en: Sally: To a certain extent, but ...
transcript.cn: 在一定程度上有帮助，但是……

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, recording is a good strategy, but I also advise you to take part in the discussion group with other fellow students to discuss the lectures and exchange ideas with them.
transcript.cn: 嗯，录音是个好办法，不过我也建议你参加同学们的讨论小组，一起讨论讲座内容并交流想法。

transcript.en: Sally: Yes, I've seen some of my classmates doing that.
transcript.cn: 是的，我见过一些同学这样做。

transcript.en: Sally: I'll ask them if I can join.
transcript.cn: 我会问问他们自己能否加入。

transcript.en: Tutor: Good, I'm sure you'll find it helps.
transcript.cn: 很好，我相信你会发现这很有帮助。

transcript.en: Tutor: And any other problems?
transcript.cn: 还有其他问题吗？

transcript.en: Sally: Yes, the second problem is, I think there aren't enough tutorials for this course.
transcript.cn: 有，第二个问题是，我觉得这个专业的辅导课不够多。

transcript.en: Tutor: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Tutor: I think you can talk to your tutor about this.
transcript.cn: 我觉得你可以和你的导师谈谈这件事。

transcript.en: Sally: Yes, I did that.
transcript.cn: 是的，我谈过了。

transcript.en: Sally: But the tutor is so busy that I must wait until she is available.
transcript.cn: 但导师太忙了，我必须等到她有空才行。

transcript.en: Tutor: Why don't you try arranging meetings ahead of time?
transcript.cn: 你为什么不试试提前预约呢？

transcript.en: Sally: Well, I can try this way.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我可以试试这个办法。

transcript.en: Tutor: Good.
transcript.cn: 很好。

transcript.en: Sally: But I suppose the thing that made me upset recently is my poor assignment grade.
transcript.cn: 不过，我想最近最让我沮丧的是作业成绩很差。

transcript.en: Tutor: Oh, the grade is an important issue.
transcript.cn: 哦，成绩确实是个重要问题。

transcript.en: Sally: Well, I think I've tried my best to achieve a good score.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我觉得自己已经尽了最大努力去取得好成绩。

transcript.en: Sally: I did more background reading than before for the last assignment we just submitted.
transcript.cn: 对于我们刚提交的上一次作业，我比以前做了更多的背景阅读。

transcript.en: Tutor: Good, and did you feel that helped?
transcript.cn: 很好，你觉得这样有帮助吗？

transcript.en: Sally: Oh yes, I think so.
transcript.cn: 哦，有的，我觉得有帮助。

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, I think you can try another strategy, which is to visit student services, which offer a lot of helpful advice on assignment preparation.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我觉得你可以尝试另一个办法，就是去学生服务中心，那里会提供许多有关作业准备的实用建议。

transcript.en: Sally: Oh, I've never heard about that.
transcript.cn: 哦，我从来没听说过。

transcript.en: Tutor: I'll give you a booklet with details on this.
transcript.cn: 我会给你一本介绍详情的小册子。

transcript.en: Tutor: You'll find it well worth trying.
transcript.cn: 你会发现这非常值得一试。

transcript.en: Sally: OK, I'll certainly try it.
transcript.cn: 好的，我一定会试试。

transcript.en: Tutor: OK, that's all the problem.
transcript.cn: 好的，问题就这些。

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, I've made a record of our discussions and suggested strategies.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我已经记录了我们讨论的内容和建议采用的方法。

transcript.en: Tutor: Typically, we advise you to try out these suggestions for a short period and then come back to discuss the situation to see whether it is improved.
transcript.cn: 通常，我们会建议你先在短期内尝试这些办法，然后再回来讨论情况，看看是否有所改善。

transcript.en: Tutor: If not, we will start considering alternative courses.
transcript.cn: 如果没有改善，我们就会开始考虑其他专业。

transcript.en: Sally: OK, when shall I come back?
transcript.cn: 好的，我什么时候再来？

transcript.en: Tutor: How about in a-month time?
transcript.cn: 一个月后怎么样？

transcript.en: Sally: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Tutor: That will be on the 17th of February.
transcript.cn: 那就是2月17日。

transcript.en: Tutor: How about half past three in the afternoon?
transcript.cn: 下午三点半怎么样？

transcript.en: Tutor: Is that all right for you?
transcript.cn: 这个时间你方便吗？

transcript.en: Sally: Yes, sure.
transcript.cn: 是的，当然。

transcript.en: Sally: And where shall I meet you?
transcript.cn: 我应该在哪里和您见面？

transcript.en: Tutor: I'm afraid I will be on leave at that time.
transcript.cn: 恐怕那时我正在休假。

transcript.en: Tutor: But I'll leave this record to our senior adviser, and you will meet with him then in this office.
transcript.cn: 不过，我会把这份记录交给我们的高级顾问，到时你会在这间办公室和他见面。

transcript.en: Sally: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Tutor: Good, that's it!
transcript.cn: 很好，就这样！

transcript.en: Tutor: Let's see whether things can improve, Sally.
transcript.cn: Sally，让我们看看情况能否有所改善。

transcript.en: Sally: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Sally: Thanks very much!
transcript.cn: 非常感谢！

transcript.en: Tutor: My pleasure.
transcript.cn: 不客气。

transcript.en: Tutor: Bye!
transcript.cn: 再见！

groups.title: Earth-sheltered Housing
groups.desc:
Questions 31-36
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect: Advantages: • has a $31$ on the environment • increased use of energy-efficient $32$ technologies • can have a very long $33$ • easy to maintain • no noise from the $34$ outside (a big problem in many conventional homes) • health benefits from advanced methods of $35$ Earth-sheltered housing in the UK: • no longer difficult to get planning permission • more likely to get approval for development in $36$ areas • may replace mobile homes on the coast

questions.title: has a 【   】 on the environment
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 advantages，提示词为 a、environment，此处应填单数名词或名词短语。
题目解析：原文开篇有一小段背景介绍，当听到“Let's first look at the advantages of earth-sheltered housing.”时，可以定位到该题。原文提到 the most significant merit，说明答案即将出现，随后听到 the low impact on the environment，对应题干信息，可判断 low impact 为正确答案。

questions.title: increased use of energy-efficient 【   】 technologies
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 energy-efficient、technologies，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：上一题结束后，听到逻辑承接词 then 时，可以定位到该题，接着相继听到题干中的 efficient 和 energy，可以准确定位。之后答案句出现“... underground housing can make great use of the alternative technologies to improve energy efficiency”，对应题干信息，可判断 alternative 为正确答案。

questions.title: can have a very long 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 a、long，此处应填单数名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，听到逻辑承接词 moreover 时，可以定位到该题。原文提到“... earth-sheltered houses have a lifespan of 500 years or more.”，其中 500 years or more 是题干 long 的同义替换，可判断 lifespan 为正确答案。

questions.title: no noise from the 【   】 outside (a big problem in many conventional homes)
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 noise，提示词为 no、the、outside，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，定位词 noise 原词重现。原文先提到“... noise is a severe problem in modern society, and many people consider moving home because of this.”，该句对应题干括号内的信息 a big problem in many conventional homes，但并未提及噪音的来源，继续往下听，可听到“... living underground means those people will no longer suffer from the disturbances caused by the traffic on the road.”，其中 no longer 对应题干否定信息，可判断 traffic 为噪音来源，符合题干要求，为正确答案。

questions.title: health benefits from advanced methods of 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 health benefits，提示词为 advanced methods、of，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，可以听到逻辑承接词 lastly 以及定位词的同义词 health advantages，接着原文提到“Because of the sophistication of the ventilation systems installed in earth-sheltered houses ...”，其中 sophistication 对应题干的 advanced methods，可判断 ventilation (systems) 为正确答案。注意，本题替换信息的抓取和答案词的拼写难度都比较大。

questions.title: more likely to get approval for development in 【   】 areas
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 planning permission、approval，提示词为 more likely、in、areas，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：通过上层信息 planning permission 辅助定位。上层信息结束后，原文提到“A lot of evidence shows that the building development offices are more sympathetic to earth-sheltered projects, especially in the conservation areas.”，其中 more sympathetic to 对应题干的 more likely to get approval，areas 后所缺内容即 conservation，因此可判断 conservation 为正确答案。

groups.question_title: Which UK projects have the following features?
groups.desc:
Questions 37-40
Which UK projects have the following features?
Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to Questions 37-40.

questions.title: favourable reaction
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 favourable reaction。
题目解析：应对这组配对题，考生需要注意以下几点：1. 选项的内容不会被替换，题干的内容会被替换，因此需要听取替换信息来定位每一道题；2. 选项的内容虽然会原词出现，但其在原文中是先于题干替换信息出现的，因此在听到选项的项目名称出现时，需要先记录下来（记录项目首字母即可）；3. 选项没有先后顺序，但答案位置的先后顺序与题号顺序基本一致，可帮助定位。本题应对方法是，上一题结束后，原文提到“In the last part, I'd like to talk about two different projects in the UK: Mole Manor and Hockerton.”，可以定位到该组配对题。先听到 Mole Manor，随后听到 attracted wide publicity around the world，对应题干 favourable reaction；接着又听到 Hockerton 和 aroused considerable interest from the international media，对应题干 favourable reaction，可判断 Mole Manor 和 Hockerton 都符合要求，因此 C 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: self-sufficiency in energy
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 energy，提示词为 self-sufficiency。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文先提到 Mole Manor，但随后提到的信息 effective insulation system、reducing heating bill 与题干信息不匹配。接着提到 Hockerton，随后提到“... all the basic needs of families living there are satisfied by energies in this area.”，后面还提到 wind power、solar energy、rain、water、electricity 等具体的能源类型，对应题干信息，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: low building costs
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 low building costs。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文提到 Hockerton，接着提到 earth-sheltered houses can be built on a modest budget，其中 built on a modest budget 对应题干的 low building costs，因此 B 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: luxurious accommodating
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 luxurious accommodating。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文提到 Mole Manor，接着提到 pays more attention to style than cost，此时还不能确定答案，需要继续往下听。原文提到“Compared with the homely cottage style at Hockerton ...”，注意句中的 homely cottage style 与 luxurious 的意思相反，可判断 Hockerton 为干扰选项。接着提到“... the houses at Mole Manor are expensive, beautiful and comfortable ... worth more than 800,000 pounds today.”，对应题干 luxurious accommodating，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。

transcript.en: For my presentation today, I will focus on earth-sheltered housing or underground housing as it's more commonly known.
transcript.cn: 今天的演讲中，我将重点介绍覆土住宅，也就是更常说的地下住宅。

transcript.en: I'll illustrate how it can meet the demands of ecological environmental planning regarding building and energy resources.
transcript.cn: 我将说明它如何在建筑和能源资源方面满足生态环境规划的要求。

transcript.en: Let's first look at the advantages of earth-sheltered housing.
transcript.cn: 我们先来看看覆土住宅的优点。

transcript.en: Perhaps the most significant merit of earth-sheltered housing is the low impact on the environment since houses built underground can hardly be seen from the surface.
transcript.cn: 覆土住宅最显著的优点或许是对环境影响很小，因为从地面上几乎看不到建在地下的房屋。

transcript.en: Then, earth-sheltered houses are not only environmentally friendly but also surprisingly efficient regarding energy.
transcript.cn: 此外，覆土住宅不仅环保，而且在能源利用方面效率高得惊人。

transcript.en: It is because underground housing can make great use of the alternative technologies to improve energy efficiency.
transcript.cn: 这是因为地下住宅可以充分利用替代技术来提高能源效率。

transcript.en: Moreover, many earth-sheltered housing projects use natural, non-toxic building materials.
transcript.cn: 此外，许多覆土住宅项目使用天然、无毒的建筑材料。

transcript.en: Some are even more durable than standard building materials.
transcript.cn: 有些材料甚至比标准建筑材料更加耐用。

transcript.en: As a result, earth-sheltered houses have a lifespan of 500 years or more.
transcript.cn: 因此，覆土住宅的使用寿命可达500年或更久。

transcript.en: This type of building technique means less exposure to the outside elements like sunshine, storm, snow etc., so it can give the houses actual protection and keep the maintenance of the houses to the minimum.
transcript.cn: 这种建筑技术意味着房屋较少暴露在阳光、暴风雨、降雪等外界自然因素中，因此能为房屋提供切实的保护，并将房屋维护需求降到最低。

transcript.en: Earth-sheltered houses also give people a quiet environment.
transcript.cn: 覆土住宅还能为人们提供安静的环境。

transcript.en: As we know, noise is a severe problem in modern society, and many people consider moving home because of this.
transcript.cn: 众所周知，噪声是现代社会的一个严重问题，许多人因此考虑搬家。

transcript.en: In that case, earth-sheltered houses might be a good choice because living underground means those people will no longer suffer from the disturbances caused by the traffic on the road.
transcript.cn: 在这种情况下，覆土住宅或许是不错的选择，因为住在地下意味着人们不再受到道路交通的干扰。

transcript.en: And lastly, earth-sheltered houses also bring significant health advantages to people.
transcript.cn: 最后，覆土住宅还会给人们的健康带来显著益处。

transcript.en: Because of the sophistication of the ventilation systems installed in earth-sheltered houses, it provides a comfortable living environment for people who have asthma and other chest diseases.
transcript.cn: 由于覆土住宅安装了先进的通风系统，它能为哮喘和其他胸部疾病患者提供舒适的居住环境。

transcript.en: Then let's move on to earth-sheltered housing in the UK.
transcript.cn: 接下来，我们来看看英国的覆土住宅。

transcript.en: Despite all the apparent advantages we mentioned above, earth-sheltered housing still isn't a popular choice for most British families, as the statistics show.
transcript.cn: 尽管它具有我们上面提到的种种明显优势，但统计数据显示，对大多数英国家庭而言，覆土住宅仍不是热门选择。

transcript.en: The British Earth-sheltering Association tries to persuade people to try this type of accommodation, saying that it's easier to get planning permission for an earth-sheltered house.
transcript.cn: 英国覆土住宅协会努力说服人们尝试这种住宅，并表示覆土住宅更容易获得规划许可。

transcript.en: In contrast, for a conventional home, there is always too much red tape.
transcript.cn: 相比之下，建造传统住宅总是有太多繁文缛节。

transcript.en: A lot of evidence shows that the building development offices are more sympathetic to earth-sheltered projects, especially in the conservation areas.
transcript.cn: 大量证据表明，建筑开发部门对覆土住宅项目更为支持，尤其是在保护区内。

transcript.en: On the sites where conventional houses aren't usually allowed to build, earth-sheltered housing might be a feasible solution.
transcript.cn: 在通常不允许建造传统住宅的地块上，覆土住宅可能是一种可行的解决方案。

transcript.en: And there is even a possibility that one day, earth-sheltered houses can take the place of mobile homes along the coastlines in the UK.
transcript.cn: 甚至有可能在未来某一天，覆土住宅会取代英国海岸线一带的活动房屋。

transcript.en: In the last part, I'd like to talk about two different projects in the UK: Mole Manor and Hockerton.
transcript.cn: 最后一部分，我想介绍英国的两个不同项目：Mole Manor和Hockerton。

transcript.en: When it was first built, Mole Manor attracted wide publicity around the world.
transcript.cn: Mole Manor刚建成时，便在世界各地引起了媒体的广泛关注。

transcript.en: A newspaper poll conducted by the Earth-sheltering Association showed that Mole Manor was voted as one of the twelve most desirable places to live in the world.
transcript.cn: 覆土住宅协会开展的一项报纸民意调查显示，Mole Manor被评为全球最理想的十二个居住地之一。

transcript.en: Similarly, Hockerton aroused considerable interest from the international media as well, winning several awards for its innovative environmental design.
transcript.cn: 同样，Hockerton也引起了国际媒体的广泛关注，并凭借创新的环保设计赢得了多个奖项。

transcript.en: The running cost for Mole Manor is much lower than that of conventional houses.
transcript.cn: Mole Manor的日常运营成本远低于传统住宅。

transcript.en: It is because Mole Manor is installed with an effective insulation system, significantly reducing its heating bill.
transcript.cn: 这是因为Mole Manor安装了高效的隔热系统，大幅降低了取暖费用。

transcript.en: While at Hockerton, all the basic needs of families living there are satisfied by energies in this area.
transcript.cn: 而在Hockerton，住户家庭的所有基本需求都由当地的能源来满足。

transcript.en: For example, wind power, solar energy and rain are used to generate enough warmth, water for washing and drinking, and electricity for lighting and cooking.
transcript.cn: 例如，利用风能、太阳能和雨水来提供充足的热量、洗涤和饮用水，以及照明和烹饪所需的电力。

transcript.en: As a result, people there can live a lifestyle in harmony with the environment.
transcript.cn: 因此，那里的人们可以过上与环境和谐共处的生活。

transcript.en: It is also proved that at Hockerton, earth-sheltered houses can be built on a modest budget because it gives priority to cost when designing the houses.
transcript.cn: 事实还证明，Hockerton的覆土住宅可以用适中的预算建成，因为房屋设计时会优先考虑成本。

transcript.en: But it is not the case with Mole Manor, which pays more attention to style than cost.
transcript.cn: 但Mole Manor并非如此，它比起成本更注重风格。

transcript.en: Compared with the homely cottage style at Hockerton, the houses at Mole Manor are expensive, beautiful and comfortable, and it is said that these houses are worth more than 800,000 pounds today.
transcript.cn: 与Hockerton朴实温馨的乡间小屋风格相比，Mole Manor的房屋昂贵、美观且舒适，据说如今这些房屋价值超过80万英镑。

transcript.en: In conclusion, Mole Manor and Hockerton Projects show that earth-sheltered housing has numerous advantages.
transcript.cn: 总而言之，Mole Manor和Hockerton项目表明，覆土住宅具有众多优点。

transcript.en: They can not only provide comfortable and stylish houses to people but are also beneficial to the environment.
transcript.cn: 它们不仅能为人们提供舒适而时尚的住宅，还有益于环境。

transcript.en: And with the help of new energy-saving technology, it can meet all the criteria for ecological and environmental planning.
transcript.cn: 借助新的节能技术，覆土住宅可以满足生态与环境规划的所有标准。
